sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + chỉ (only) + ăn (eat) + hai (two) + bát (bowls) + cơm (rice)
Anh (he) + chỉ (only) + uống (drink) + hai (two) + ly (glasses) + nước (water)
Chị (she) + chỉ (only) + mua (buy) + một (one) + cái (classifier) + áo (shirt)
notes_md:
**chỉ** means **only / just**. It limits something — number, time, person, action, etc.

It usually goes **before the verb or the quantity** it limits.  

It narrows the focus: “no more than that.” Think of it as drawing a small circle around the important part.

Very common synonym: **chỉ có** (“there is only…”), slightly more explicit.

definitions.en: Used to emphasize that something is the only thing or is limited.
definitions.vi: Dùng để nhấn mạnh rằng điều gì đó là duy nhất hoặc giới hạn.

example.en: I just want to help you.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I only eat two bowls of rice.
He only drinks two glasses of water.
exampleSentences: Em chỉ ăn hai bát cơm.
Anh chỉ uống hai ly nước.
Chị chỉ mua một cái áo.
notes:
